votes.reason: Both functions check if the given string 'res' is the concatenation of the list of strings.
The Python code correctly checks if the result string is the concatenation of the input list of strings, matching the Coq specification.
Both functions check if the given string 'res' is the concatenation of the list of strings 'strings'.

Both compute string concatenation of a list. Coq's fold_right with String.append and EmptyString concatenates strings left-to-right (first element first), which matches Python's "".join(strings). For any concrete list of strings, both produce the same concatenated result.

Both compute string concatenation of a list. Coq's fold_right with String.append and EmptyString concatenates strings left-to-right (first element first), which matches Python's "".join(strings) behavior. For any concrete list of strings, both produce the same concatenated result.
Both compute the ordered concatenation of the list with empty string as identity; Python "".join(strings) matches Coq fold_right String.append EmptyString.
Both compute the concatenation of the list of strings in order with empty string as the identity; fold_right String.append EmptyString matches "".join(strings).
Both define the result as the concatenation of the list of strings in order (empty list yields empty string), so they match on all inputs.
